title: Authentication is not authorization
description: Knowing who a caller is does not prove what object, action, tenant, or state they may access.
body: Use when code checks login state, token validity, session presence, or user ID but then reads or writes protected resources. Do not use to design the full permission taxonomy. Actions: look for a separate policy decision tied to action and resource; require owner, role, tenant, and state checks where relevant. Validation signals include tests where authenticated users are denied outside their scope. Risk boundary: replacing authorization with authentication is a blocking issue for protected resources.
